title: rourea neglecta
intro: Rourea neglecta, also known as the "Strychnine Bush," is a plant species native to Australia and New Zealand. It contains alkaloids, including the toxic compound strychnine, but it also possesses medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is used in herbal preparations for various health benefits, including relieving pain, reducing fever, and treating skin conditions. However, due to its toxicity, the plant must be handled with caution, and herbal preparations should only be administered under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
